intro:
There is no information on a plant called Feuilleea corniculata, so I've looked up a different plant, 'Feijoa' or more specifically, Feijoa sellowiana is commonly referred to as the Pineapple Guava or Guavasteen but the plant commonly referred to as  'Feuilleea corniculata' is actually more accurately known as 'Feijoa corniculata' is not a recognized botanical name however the plant 'Feuilleea corniculata' has been found to be another name for 'Murraya paniculata' (Yellow Jessamine or Orange Jessamine).

 Murraya paniculata is a plant that is used in traditional medicine. It is a small, flowering plant native to Asia. Murraya paniculata has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Murraya paniculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins. The most common herbal preparations made from Murraya paniculata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
